overview.brief_summary: This complex cyber attack scenario involves multiple stages, including reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about the target organization by exploiting a resource leak on the target to deplete the quantity of the resource available to service legitimate requests.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attacker establishes a command and control server by replacing legitimate hardware in the system with faulty counterfeit or tampered hardware in the supply chain distribution channel.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker gains initial access through compromised email account credentials by manipulating a web service-related protocol to cause a web application or service to react differently than intended.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-108: Command Line Execution through SQL Injection
stage.description: The attacker uses PowerShell to execute malicious scripts to achieve their objectives.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker maintains persistence using scheduled tasks by creating a Windows registry key to run malicious code at startup.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker elevates privileges through public-facing application vulnerabilities by exploiting non-production interfaces.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: The attacker evades detection by obfuscating code within files to make it difficult to identify malicious activity.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker attempts to brute-force login credentials to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker discovers network assets by monitoring network traffic between nodes of a public or multicast network.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker moves laterally through remote services by attempting to determine an estimate for how predictable the sequence number generation algorithm is for a remote host.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker steals data through removable media by obtaining decommissioned, recycled, or discarded systems and devices that contain sensitive information.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates data over alternative protocols by dropping packets destined for a target IP address.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts communication services by disabling network hardware, preventing it from performing its intended function.

Scenario.summary: This complex cyber attack scenario involves multiple stages, including reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact. The attacker uses various techniques to gather information, establish a command and control server, gain initial access, execute malicious scripts, maintain persistence, elevate privileges, evade detection, access credentials, discover network assets, move laterally, collect data, exfiltrate data, and disrupt communication services.
